steps.title: Identify what the example must demonstrate
steps.content: The sentence to complete reads: "For some species, more fish were collected in the winter than the other two seasons, for instance: ______" The example must name at least one specific species and show its winter count exceeding both its spring count and its autumn count, using data from the graph.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, keep the one that correctly demonstrates winter dominance
steps.content:
Check each choice against the graph data and the claim:
  • Choice A — more common silver-biddy and milkfish were collected in the winter than in either of the other two seasons.: common silver-biddy (winter: 35, spring: 17, autumn: 10) and milkfish (winter: 12, spring: 7, autumn: 0) — both show winter exceeding the other seasons. ✓
  • Choice B — the common silver-biddy was collected more frequently than the other two species in all three seasons.: compares species against each other within seasons, not a single species across seasons — wrong comparison. ✗
  • Choice C — in the spring, researchers collected more Red Sea goldfish than they collected from the other two species.: describes spring, not winter, and compares across species rather than across seasons. ✗
  • Choice D — in the fall, researchers collected 10 common silver-biddy but collected no milkfish or Red Sea goatfish.: describes autumn data, not winter dominance. ✗
A is correct.
